prediction.reasoning: Based on typical stock volatility patterns for large-cap financial stocks like Visa, I estimated a 22-day forecast using an annualized volatility of approximately 25%. The point estimate assumes slight positive drift typical of established blue-chip stocks. Confidence intervals are constructed using normal distribution assumptions with volatility scaled to the 22-day time horizon (√22/252 ≈ 0.295 years). The intervals widen appropriately with higher confidence levels to account for increased uncertainty over the forecast period.
